spec_match.rationale: Composes `Iterator.concat(...)` with map / filter / take / drop helpers across six source types (array, Set, generator, Map.entries, string, infinite generator) and an eager-spread alternative â€” exactly the TC39 proposal's compositional story.

interactive.rationale: Live source picker (order matters), helper toggles, lazy-vs-eager strategy toggle, per-source pull counters, and a 'eager would hang' warning when an infinite source is included â€” the richest concept in this folder.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises finite/infinite/heterogeneous sources, chained helpers, lazy-vs-eager, per-source pull counts, and an eager-hang warning â€” all delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Infinite-source-after-finite is the canonical test for lazy concat, and the page leads with that exact framing. Eager mode is correctly time-boxed or pre-checked to avoid hanging. Multi-source ordering is exposed as a control. Strong edge-case coverage.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5193275975794688 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: Variables-driven, opt toggles use class-based on/off states, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add `flatMap` / `reduce` helpers
openQuestions.detail: The TC39 proposal includes the full iterator-helper suite (`flatMap`, `reduce`, `every`, `some`, `find`, `forEach`). The builder currently exposes only the four most common helpers. Add the remaining three or four so the page covers the full proposal surface.

openQuestions.title: Surface the .return() cleanup hook
openQuestions.detail: When `.take(5)` stops, the spec calls `.return()` on the underlying iterators so generators can run their `finally` blocks. Add a 'show generator cleanup' toggle that wires `try { ... } finally { log('cleanup') }` into one of the generator sources so authors see the cleanup contract.

openQuestions.title: Add a stream-like source
openQuestions.detail: Real applications wrap a `ReadableStream` (or an EventTarget) as an async iterable. While `Iterator.concat()` is sync-only, the page should explicitly note that and ideally provide a parallel demo or link to the async counterpart so visitors aren't surprised when they try to pipe an event source through.

summary: Strongest interactive surface for this feature â€” lazy vs eager, per-source pull counters, infinite + finite composition. To complete coverage, add the remaining iterator helpers and the `.return()` cleanup story.
